response:
Nurse Emily organizes a creative writing workshop for the older adults in her care facility, aiming to provide a therapeutic and engaging activity. With 24 residents interested in joining, she divides them into groups of 6 participants each. This ensures that each group receives personalized attention and support. 

Emily plans to allocate 3 writing prompts per group for the session. Since there are 4 groups (24 residents ÷ 6 participants per group = 4 groups), she will need 4 × 3 = 12 initial writing prompts. 

To accommodate the possibility that groups may want to continue writing, Emily prepares an additional set of 10 extra prompts. These prompts are shared equally among all the groups, meaning each group will receive 10 ÷ 4 = 2.5 prompts, which is not practical. Therefore, the 10 extra prompts are kept as a shared resource for the entire session. 

In total, Emily needs to prepare 12 initial prompts plus 10 extra prompts, resulting in 22 writing prompts. The library at the facility will be used to store these prompts, and the library also serves as a resource for other activities. 

The END.
